title: Avoiding re-render via store subscription

performance_problem.metric_impact: Track render_count_per_action: React Profiler shows only components whose inputs changed re-render.
performance_problem.business_impact: If Avoiding re-render via store subscription regresses, users see slower completion or retries; track support contacts, abandonment, and the technical metric together.

reasoning: perf-061 treats Avoiding re-render via store subscription as a react_rendering bottleneck. The fix is valid only when React Profiler, browser INP trace, render-count assertions, and interaction replay show render_count_per_action (count) meeting the row target: only components with changed inputs re-render; correctness output must remain unchanged and the rollback path must be explicit.
solution:
import {useShallow} from 'zustand/react/shallow';
const {a,b} = useStore(useShallow(s => ({a: s.a, b: s.b})));

after_metrics: perf-061 optimized result for Avoiding re-render via store subscription: render_count_per_action must meet target (only components with changed inputs re-render), with no correctness diff, no adjacent-metric regression, and artifacts attached to the CI or review record.
trade_offs: Avoiding re-render via store subscription adds state-boundary and memoization discipline; keep the component model readable and remove the optimization if Profiler evidence disappears.
regression_prevention: React Profiler fixture for Avoiding re-render via store subscription asserts render_count_per_action; browser INP budget and render-count snapshots fail the pull request on regression.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive fix for Avoiding re-render via store subscription changes the nearest slow line without measuring render_count_per_action. title: Reducing render-blocking CSS

performance_problem.metric_impact: Track p75_lcp_ms: p75 LCP improves on slow 4G without increasing CLS or INP.
performance_problem.business_impact: If Reducing render-blocking CSS regresses, users see slower completion or retries; track support contacts, abandonment, and the technical metric together.

reasoning: perf-064 treats Reducing render-blocking CSS as a core_web_vitals bottleneck. The fix is valid only when Lighthouse trace, Chrome performance recording, and web-vitals RUM sample show p75_lcp_ms (milliseconds) meeting the row target: p75 LCP improves without worsening CLS or INP; correctness output must remain unchanged and the rollback path must be explicit.
solution:
<head>
  <style>/* critical above-fold CSS inlined */</style>
  <link rel="preload" href="/main.css" as="style" onload="this.onload=null;this.rel='stylesheet'"/>
  <noscript><link rel="stylesheet" href="/main.css"/></noscript>
</head>
